evals.prompt: I just fixed a null pointer bug in the user service, can you write a commit message for me? The staged changes are a 3-line fix in src/services/UserService.php adding a null check.
evals.expected_output: A concise commit message with fix type, appropriate scope, imperative subject, and brief body. Should NOT use the full Summary/Why/How structure since it's a small change.
expectations: Message is appropriately brief for a small change (no full Summary/Why/How sections)
No AI attribution or Co-Authored-By line

evals.expected_output: The skill should trigger even with this minimal prompt, analyze staged changes via git commands, and produce a well-structured commit message.
expectations: Runs git diff --staged or equivalent to analyze changes
Runs git log to check repository conventions
Produces a commit message with conventional commit type
Subject line uses imperative mood and is under 72 characters

evals.prompt: I've been working on adding Redis caching to our product API, plus I refactored the database connection pool and fixed a typo in the README. Can you write commit messages for all of this?
evals.expected_output: Should suggest splitting into 2-3 separate commits since these are unrelated concerns. Should draft messages for each.
expectations: Provides a feat-type message for the caching feature
Provides a refactor-type message for the database changes
Provides a docs-type message for the README fix
